ip_outcome_note: The flavor text 'Alakazam, alakazook, send this page back to its book!' is attributed to Merlin on the Mushu Report Lorcana wiki (wiki.mushureport.com/wiki/Merlin_(character)). The vision description — elderly robed figure, white beard, wand raised in commanding gesture, banishing a book — matches Merlin from The Sword in the Stone (1963) precisely. Merlin is a Disney-owned IP (not an external crossover); ip_confidence raised to high. No external IP risk flag needed.

trivia_bullets.text: Whispers in the Well is the tenth Disney Lorcana set, with a pre-release November 7, 2025, and worldwide release November 14, 2025. The set's theme is film noir, mystery, and supernatural, taking players into the Ink Well — the in-universe source of all Glimmers — where ghostly 'Whisper' glimmers have emerged. A new mechanic, Boost, allows Whisper-type characters to place the top card of their deck facedown beneath them to unlock abilities. `[Mushu Report Lorcana Wiki: Whispers in the Well; MiceChat: Set 10 revealed]`
sources: Mushu Report Lorcana Wiki: Whispers in the Well
MiceChat.com: Whispers in the Well Set 10 reveal

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Alakazam, alakazook, send this page back to its book!" — Merlin. The incantation is a rhyming banishment spell in the style of Merlin's whimsical verbal magic from The Sword in the Stone (1963 Disney film). The card's oracle text — return a character, item, or location with cost 3 or less to their player's hand — directly mechanizes the concept of magical banishment. `[Card frontmatter; Mushu Report wiki: Merlin (character)]`

Begone! is a 3-cost Amethyst Action that returns a chosen character, item, or location with cost 3 or less to their player's hand — a tempo-removal spell effective in the early and mid-game. Amethyst is the ink color most associated with Lorcana's magical/sorcery archetype; bounce effects in Amethyst are a recurring mechanical identity. `[Card frontmatter; TCGPlayer: Begone! product listing]`

Illustrated by Agathe Molin. Merlin from The Sword in the Stone (1963) has the largest card presence of any Sword in the Stone character in Lorcana — 11 named Merlin cards across sets 1–10 (confirmed in Mushu Report's character page), spanning Amethyst and Sapphire ink. 'Begone!' (set 10) joins a tradition of Merlin-attributed action cards that began in set 1 with cards like 'Develop Your Brain' and 'Befuddle.' `[Mushu Report wiki: Merlin (character); Mushu Report wiki: Begone!]`
